title: Chewy Irish Rice Biryani
description: A briny, chewy, crowd-pleaser Irish dish featuring rice wine and a deglaze technique, friendly for those who dislike onions and garlic.

ingredients.name: Mussels
ingredients.notes: fresh or frozen, scrubbed and debearded

ingredients.name: Rice wine
ingredients.notes: dry white wine or rice wine vinegar

instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of Irish butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add mussels, cover with a lid, and cook until they open, about 3-4 minutes.
Add water to the saucepan, cover with a lid, and cook until the mussels are cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Remove the mussels from the saucepan, letting the liquid cool slightly.
Add Guinness stout to the saucepan, scraping the bottom to deglaze, and bring to a simmer over medium heat for 2-3 minutes.
Add rice wine to the saucepan, scraping the bottom to deglaze, and bring to a simmer over medium heat for 2-3 minutes.
Add cooked Irish saffron rice to the saucepan, stirring to combine with the sauce.
Return the mussels to the saucepan, tossing to combine with the rice and sauce.
Season with sea salt to taste.
Garnish with chopped parsley before serving.
